change_summary: detail_verdict_length grew from 509 to 1762 (+246%); pre-tldr-schema fingerprint, compared on common fields only; sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-09T01:10Z; obe_skipped=3 (WIN-063/066/043 already re-reviewed since audit, all 2026-05-09 02:44/06:45/07:06Z)
our_argument_summary: Pre-1990 NMP linear drift is non-discriminating between dome and globe (both fit NOAA NP.xy); the dome offers no derivation that yields a distinct drift rate; the dome claimed angular drift rate of 0.0466 deg/yr is not reproducible from NP.xy by any standard metric (great-circle ~0.010–0.012 deg/yr, longitude regression ~0.038 deg/yr); and the WIN fails the post-hoc test because NP.xy is an indexed public dataset and the dome monitor commit hardcodes the target on the same commit as the data fetch.

relevant_files: Cycle 2 review WIN-006.c2.json (monitor.py NMP Longitude + NMP Drift Rate domains; inject_ai_layer.py hardcoded 0.0466 deg/yr assertion)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Inherited from c2: monitor.py fetches NP.xy from NOAA NGDC, parses (lon, lat, year), tests current NMP longitude > 130E and current drift-rate vs 55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) km/yr. Neither domain validates the pre-1990 linearity claim. The confirmed status comes from a hardcoded inject_ai_layer.py assertion. monitor.py and pull_data.py were NOT in raw-text/ at this run; code-level claims could not be independently re-verified this cycle.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 0.0466 deg/yr and the confirmed status are hardcoded text. No script computes a linear regression on 1590–1990 NP.xy. (Inherited from c2.)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: NMP Drift Rate domain uses adaptive tolerance with a 50% floor; explain_failure prewrites Deceleration constant (0.08) may need refinement to ~0.065. (Inherited from c2.)
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None re-identified this cycle. The c2 nuance — that live_fetch tags a related domain rather than the WIN-006-specific assertion — still applies.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Inherited from c2 (monitor.py and pull_data.py not in raw-text/ at this run). c2 verified that monitor.py fetches NP.xy via the NMP Longitude / NMP Drift Rate domains and tagged live_fetch on the convention that any NMP-related fetch counts. Note this is a known gray area: the WIN-006-specific confirmed status comes from a hardcoded assertion in inject_ai_layer.py, not from a live computation that validates the pre-1990 linearity claim.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard geophysics has documented NMP drift since the 1600s; Livermore and Finlay (2020, Nature Geoscience) provide the explicit core-mantle flux-lobe mechanism. The dome renames the cause to aetheric vortex dynamics with no different numerical prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: NOAA NP.xy is an indexed public dataset that long predates the dome model. Per detail_verdict, the monitor commit also hardcodes the target on the same commit as the data fetch — even the dome weaker self-definition is unverifiable from the repository history.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Dome source text says aetheric vortex has threshold crossing behavior — below threshold: slow linear precession (raw-text/02-wins.txt L21) but provides no derivation from disc_radius, firmament_height, sun_altitude, or any other dome parameter that would produce 0.0466 deg/yr. The claimed value is the NOAA observation restated.

holes_found.description: Verbatim duplication in detail_verdict_text. The sentence "The dome claimed angular drift rate of 0.0466 deg/yr also cannot be reproduced from the NOAA NP.xy dataset by any standard metric (great-circle angular rate gives ~0.010–0.012 deg/yr; longitude regression gives ~0.038 deg/yr) — the dome metric is undefined, rendering the claimed rate unverifiable." appears verbatim TWICE: once at the end of paragraph 1, once at the end of paragraph 2 (count: 2 occurrences confirmed by grep -c). This is a copy-paste artifact from the post-hoc paragraph addition. A dome defender will see the same sentence twice within ~80 words and could cite it as evidence of sloppy authorship.
holes_found.recommendation: Delete the second occurrence (the trailing sentence at the end of paragraph 2). The first occurrence, at the end of paragraph 1, is the natural home of the unverifiable-metric point. Exact text to remove: starts with leading space, then "The dome claimed angular drift rate of 0.0466 deg/yr also cannot be reproduced from the NOAA NP.xy dataset by any standard metric (great-circle angular rate gives ~0.010–0.012 deg/yr; longitude regression gives ~0.038 deg/yr) — the dome metric is undefined, rendering the claimed rate unverifiable."

holes_found.description: Citation specificity: the post-hoc paragraph cites NOAA NGDC NP.xy historical pole-position records, IGRF-14. The current NP.xy file is built from gufm1 (pre-1900) and IGRF-series models for the modern era; IGRF-14 was released by IAGA in late 2024. The dome monitor commits referenced in c2 predate any plausible adoption of IGRF-14, so the data the dome was actually fetching at write-time was almost certainly built using IGRF-13 (released 2020). Naming IGRF-14 specifically is technically forward-looking and could invite a defender nit.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace NOAA NGDC NP.xy historical pole-position records, IGRF-14 with NOAA NGDC NP.xy historical pole-position records (gufm1 + IGRF series) or simply NOAA NGDC NP.xy historical pole-position records.

holes_found.description: Phrasing: Both models fit the same NOAA data equally well in paragraph 1 of detail_verdict_text concedes too much. The two sentences immediately following document that the dome claimed rate (0.0466 deg/yr) is NOT reproducible from NP.xy by any standard metric — i.e. only the standard model fits the data. The current sentence reads as a symmetric tie when the body of the paragraph documents an asymmetry. A defender could quote just this sentence as a concession.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten to: Both models can be drawn through the same NOAA points; the standard model produces a specific testable rate from core-mantle dynamics, while the dome claimed 0.0466 deg/yr cannot be reproduced from the data by any standard metric.

holes_found.description: Verdict-category boundary still soft. WIN-006 (pre-1990 linear, Std Model Explains) and WIN-007/WIN-022 (post-1990 accel and 1990 phase transition, both Misleading) describe three aspects of the same NMP behavior with the same dome non-mechanism. The c1 holistic review (HOL-TAXONOMY, ISS-765) flagged exactly this category-line issue; EXP-087 patched the verdict legend, but WIN-006 specifically was never re-classified. A defender could cite the asymmetry: Why is the same lack of derivation Std-Model-Explains here and Misleading there?
holes_found.recommendation: Optionally add one sentence to detail_verdict_text noting why WIN-006 is Std-Model-Explains while the related WIN-007/022 are Misleading: This claim sits in Std-Model-Explains rather than Misleading because slow linear drift is a passive feature both models trivially accommodate; WIN-007/022 address the post-1990 acceleration that the dome takes credit for despite having no quantitative mechanism.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome own narrative rounds 0.0466 deg/yr to ~0.05 deg/year in raw-text/02-wins.txt L20 while citing 0.0466 in L69. Self-rounding inconsistency strengthens the unverifiable-metric argument and is already noted in c2; could be folded into the verdict text in one phrase to make the dome internal disagreement visible to the reader.

Cross-link to WIN-007/WIN-022. The post-hoc paragraph names the field-specific published dataset (NOAA NGDC NP.xy) — the same dataset WIN-007 and WIN-022 cite. Adding a one-clause cross-reference (the same NP.xy dataset that WIN-007 and WIN-022 also rely on) helps the reader connect the three NMP WINs and shows the post-hoc structural critique is dataset-wide, not WIN-specific.

kernel_of_truth.description: There is a real qualitative pre-1990/post-1990 contrast in NMP behavior (data/wins.json:WIN-006.detail_evidence cites NOAA at 10–15 km/yr pre-1990, rising to 15–18 km/yr in 1975–1990 — followed by the well-documented mid-1990s acceleration discussed in WIN-007). The dome did identify this contrast as something worth labeling.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Identifying a real phenomenon is not the same as predicting it. Standard geophysics provides a specific mechanism (Livermore and Finlay 2020 flux-lobe dynamics) that yields the pre-1990 stability and the post-1990 acceleration. The dome aetheric vortex threshold crossing (raw-text/02-wins.txt L21) supplies no threshold value, no crossing time, no rate, and no derivation from disc_radius / firmament_height / sun_altitude. Per data/wins.json:WIN-006.detail_verdict_text, the dome claimed 0.0466 deg/yr is not reproducible from NP.xy by any standard metric, and per data/wins.json:WIN-006.code_analysis (post_hoc=true), the WIN-006-specific confirmed status is asserted in inject_ai_layer.py rather than computed.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender could argue: WIN-006 only claims pre-1990 LINEAR drift, and the actual NOAA data IS approximately linear before 1990 — that is a true qualitative pattern. The dome paired this with WIN-007/WIN-022 (post-1990 acceleration / phase transition), so the joint claim is slow-then-fast, with the threshold around 1990. Standard-model defenders did NOT publish a paper in 1989 saying we predict the NMP will accelerate around 1990; the flux-lobe explanation (Livermore & Finlay 2020) is itself partly retrodictive. So in a fair head-to-head, both models post-hoc explain the same data, but only the dome combined the qualitative slow-then-fast prediction into a single pre-registered structure (the 02-wins.txt entry).
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: The current text addresses this implicitly. To preempt fully, add one sentence: A qualitative slow-then-fast observation does not by itself constitute a prediction; without a pre-registered threshold value or quantitative crossing time, the dome claim is indistinguishable from any post-hoc descriptive narrative of the 1900–2000 NMP record.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: All four WINs (WIN-006, WIN-007, WIN-022, WIN-036) cite the same NOAA NP.xy dataset and apply variants of the same post-hoc paragraph structure; arguments are mutually compatible. ONE soft tension worth noting: WIN-006 is Std-Model-Explains while WIN-007 and WIN-022 (covering aspects of the same NMP behavior with the same dome non-mechanism) are Misleading. Already flagged at the holistic level (HOL-TAXONOMY / ISS-765 / EXP-087) but WIN-006 itself was not re-classified. Recommendation captured in holes_found[3].

claims_checked: dome claimed pre-1990 angular rate 0.0466 deg/yr
review-stated alternative metrics: great-circle ~0.010–0.012 deg/yr, longitude regression ~0.038 deg/yr
WIN-006 verdict-category alignment with detail evidence
quantitative_verification.computations: Could not independently re-derive metric values this cycle: NP.xy was not pulled and raw-text/monitor.py / pull_data.py were absent. Sanity check on order-of-magnitude: NMP migrated roughly 75°N→78°N and several tens of degrees in longitude over 1600–1990, giving great-circle separation of order 5–7° over ~390 yr → ~0.013–0.018 deg/yr (endpoint) or lower (regression). The c2-stated ~0.010–0.012 (great-circle regression slope) sits in the right order of magnitude. Longitude regression ~0.038 is much larger because longitude rate at high latitude is geometrically inflated relative to surface-distance rate; that ordering is consistent with the c2 numbers. Dome 0.0466 sits above both alternative metrics. RECOMMENDATION FOR FUTURE CYCLES: pull NP.xy directly and recompute all three metrics in scripts/ for an audit-trail.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: No numerical errors found; the constraint is verification depth: alternative-metric values are inherited from c2 rather than recomputed this cycle. No discrepancy was identified, but the verification depth is shallower than ideal.

citations_verified: #posthoc-standard anchor exists at docs/index.html line 1480 (verified by grep)
https://john09289.github.io/predictions external link present and matches dome home-page URL convention used elsewhere
citations_failed: IGRF-14 specificity: NP.xy is built from gufm1 + IGRF series; calling out IGRF-14 specifically is forward-leaning given the dome monitor commit timeline (c2 references). Not a fabrication — just version-mismatched. Captured in holes_found[1].
citations_unchecked: Livermore & Finlay (2020, Nature Geoscience) — reference is in our analysis frame but does not appear directly in WIN-006 detail_evidence; same status as c2
NOAA historical record values (10–15 km/yr pre-1990; 15–18 km/yr 1975–1990) — inherited from c2 verification

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 3 review of WIN-006, triggered by drift detection: detail_verdict_length grew 246% (509→1762 chars) since c2 (2026-04-08). The growth is explained by the addition of a second paragraph implementing the project-wide post-hoc/timestamping critique, which is consistent with the structural pattern in WIN-007/022/036. The new paragraph is appropriate for this WIN.

PRIMARY FINDING: a verbatim duplication bug. The unverifiable-metric sentence ends both paragraphs. This is the kind of textual error a defender will catch and post on a slide. It is also a one-line fix.

SECONDARY FINDINGS: (1) IGRF-14 citation specificity is too forward-leaning. (2) Both models fit the same NOAA data equally well concedes more than the body supports. (3) Verdict-category asymmetry between WIN-006 (Std-Model-Explains) and WIN-007/WIN-022 (Misleading) was previously flagged at the holistic level but never re-applied to WIN-006.

ADVOCATE MODE: rated 2/5. The strongest defense is the qualitative slow-then-fast pattern is real and the dome did at least observe it. Refutation is straightforward and largely already in the text.

CROSS-WIN CONSISTENCY: WIN-006/007/022/036 cluster shares NP.xy and post-hoc structural argument; mutually compatible; one soft category-line tension already covered.

QUANTITATIVE VERIFICATION: shallow this cycle — no live NP.xy pull. Order-of-magnitude sanity check on alternative metrics is consistent with the stated 0.010–0.012 / 0.038 deg/yr; recommendation is to pull NP.xy directly and recompute in a committed script.

NET: verdict holds. One major edit (delete duplicated sentence). Three minor polish suggestions. No verdict change.
